frameworks.name: hybrid marginal-joint credit assignment
frameworks.one_line: arbitration between cue-independent marginal updating and cue-coupled joint updating

frameworks.name: probabilistic cue combination
frameworks.one_line: classic multi-cue task reframed through curriculum-shaped learning trajectories

frameworks.name: curriculum learning
frameworks.one_line: divide-and-conquer training schedules that decompose problems into parts

core_question: How do different training curricula shape human learning in a probabilistic cue combination task?
key_assumptions: learners can deploy either marginal or joint credit-assignment strategies
an arbitration process selects between the two updating processes
curriculum order modulates which strategy dominates
manipulations: curriculum structure (single-cue vs multi-cue presentation order)
skewed-distribution multi-cue curricula predicted to help or hinder
test trials involving multiple cues after differing training
key_findings: curricula presenting one cue at a time improve later multi-cue test performance
a hybrid arbitration model captures the curriculum-dependent learning effect
model-predicted skewed curricula succeed or fail to promote learning as forecast
interpretations: human curriculum benefit reflects arbitration between marginal and joint credit assignment
computational theory can be used to design curricula that accelerate probabilistic learning
